enrichment.note: Retraction notice with no quantitative results; nothing recomputable. c1/c3 reviewed as CONFIRMED-CORRECT (qualitative T1, no over-reach); c2 integrity signal kept as T7 route + advisory; abstained shrunk to the genuinely-unassessable (empty).

routed_to_human.note: Reviewed across 5 perspectives, no over-reach. c1 is a T1 bibliographic claim stating the retracted article's title/journal/DOI (Exp Ther Med 20:1431-1440, 2020; DOI 10.3892/etm.2020.8856) -- it merely transcribes the masthead of the document being audited (a retraction notice), so the claim cannot exceed its evidence. SKEPTIC: the only failure mode is a transcription error in the title/DOI, and ev1's quote matches the claim verbatim. CLAIMS-AUDITOR: claim strength (qualitative existence) == evidence strength; no quantitative result is asserted (the document reports none). No numeric verifier covers it yet -- abstain dropped as CONFIRMED-CORRECT, not silently.

routed_to_human.note: DOMAIN-EXPERT (biomedical literature) / would-confirm: the original DOI 10.3892/etm.2020.8856 and its retracted status are externally checkable as a T1 fixed-knowledge lookup (Crossref / Retraction Watch DB / PubMed retraction flag). LITMUS has no citation-exists/retracted verifier in the library yet (DESIGN §5 T1, §19 WS-D), so this stays advisory rather than a scored verdict. Confirming step: resolve the DOI and assert Crossref 'update-to' / RW lists it as retracted; that would promote c1 from advisory to a deterministic_confirmed T1 verdict.

routed_to_human.note: INTEGRITY-SCREENER (T7): this is the stated grounds for retraction -- an immunohistochemical image (Fig. 1C, p. 1434) and western-blot panels (Figs. 3B, 4B) reported by a concerned reader as strikingly similar to data previously published by different authors at different institutes (i.e. cross-paper image reuse / duplication). Per DESIGN §3.5 and §5 T7, image-duplication is a screening signal surfaced to a human, explicitly NOT scored: LITMUS ships no image-forensics verifier and the source figures are not in this corpus, so code cannot settle it. SKEPTIC note: the notice itself reports the allegation (it does not independently re-prove the overlap), and the authors did not reply -- so the integrity concern is corroborated by an editorial decision but the pixel-level match remains human-verifiable. Would-confirm: side-by-side panel overlay / copy-move + ELA forensics against the named prior articles, or a PubPeer thread documenting the duplicated source panels.

routed_to_human.note: Reviewed across 5 perspectives, no over-reach; no numeric verifier covers it yet. c3 is a T1 procedural claim about the editorial process (Editor decided to retract; authors asked for an explanation; no reply received) -- a direct transcription of the notice's own statement, so claim cannot exceed evidence (ev1). METHODOLOGIST (T3): n/a -- the document reports no study method or numeric result to assess. CLAIMS-AUDITOR (T4): no causal or quantitative over-claim; 'did not receive a reply' is an absence-of-event the Editorial Office is the authoritative source for. SKEPTIC: weakest link is whether 'no reply' is literally true, which is unfalsifiable from outside the editorial office and not a paper defect. Abstain dropped as CONFIRMED-CORRECT, made auditable here rather than silently.

routed_to_human.note: ROUTE-TO-HUMAN (DESIGN §3.5, §5 T8): whether the (now-retracted) finding that Oblongifolin C reverses gemcitabine resistance by suppressing autophagy flux in bladder cancer would be significant/important *if true* is irreducibly subjective and is explicitly not scored. Surfaced for completeness; moot given the retraction, but recorded so the dimension is routed rather than silently judged.
